title: [Feature]: Cache the result of deserialization when loading ref
body:
When loading external $ref with swagger-parser, the ResolverCache stores only the read string but still deserialize the whole tree which is particuraly slow for big files.
Maybe the Resolver could also store the deserialized tree.

title: [Bug]: External schema resolution broken in OpenAPI 3.1 (works in 3.0)
body: When parsing an OpenAPI 3.1 specification with external schema references (using `$ref` to separate YAML files), the `getComponents().getSchemas()` method returns `null`.

title: [Bug]: Validation behavior change between openapi-generator 7.13.0 and 7.14.0 against an OpenAPI 3.1 schema with external references
body: Validation behavior against an ISO spec (SOVD, an OpenAPI 3.1 based specification) fails after openapi-generator 7.14.0.

title: BDSA-2018-5289 Mozilla Rhino - 1.7.7.2
body: Mozilla Rhino is vulnerable to XML external entities (XXE) due to an insecure XML parsing in the toXml function.

title: [Bug]: allOf reference not resolved fully
body: Setting `resolveFully(true)` does not resolve `$ref` pointers inside `allOf` schemas.

title: [Bug]: x-nullable lost for shared $ref responses during Swagger 2.0 to OAS3 conversion
body: When converting Swagger 2.0 specifications to OpenAPI 3.0, the `x-nullable` vendor extension is lost for the second and subsequent endpoints when multiple endpoints share the same response definition via `$ref`.

title: [bug] For type: date parsed wrong example
body: For type: date parsed wrong example

title: [Bug]: OpenAPI 3.1 Parser Unexpectedly Merges Identical Inline Objects When resolve=true
body: When parsing an OpenAPI 3.1 specification with `ParseOptions.setResolve(true)`, the `OpenAPIDereferencer31` unexpectedly merges identical inline objects.

title: [Bug]: Parameters components shouldn't be inlined with resolve option set to true
body: Following the doc, the `resolve` option should resolve refs (for parameters usage) from other external/relative sources/files and **not** replace ref usage by the inline version of the parameter.

title: [Feature]: Support for OpenAPI Spec 3.2
body: Add support for parsing and validating OpenAPI Specification 3.2.0.

title: [Bug]: References to elements within jar files cannot be resolved
body: When an openapi spec is contained in a jar file and read using a jar URI, references to elements within that spec cannot be resolved.

title: [Feature]: Upgrade to Jackson 3
body: I have swagger parser as dependency and this provides Jackson 2 as transient dependencies. I would like to get rid of Jackson 2 dependencies.

title: additionalProperties inside ComposedSchema are resolved as null
body: When I parse an OpenAPI document, additionalProperties inside ComposedSchema are resolved as null.

title: [Feature]: Allow customization of Accept-header In RemoteUrl
body: Currently, the Accept-header is hard coded in `RemoteUrl`. Users can't change it, even if their Api might require different headers.

title: [Question] [Bug]: Inconsistent Relative Reference Resolution in Nested Schema Contexts
body: There seems to be a bug when resolving nested schemas. OpenAPI Generator incorrectly resolves relative $ref paths in schema files when they are referenced through nested contexts.

title: [Question]: Duplicate _1 Object generation due to different path prefix
body: I'm trying to parse 2 API Specifications in OpenAPI 3.0.3 using OpenApiGenerator version 7.1.0. This results in BrokenTO and BrokenTO_1 references.

title: Duplicated referenced definitions
body: I have the following swagger files with referenced definitions that create duplicates.

title: External ref resolve fails to resolve to same schema and creates duplicate classes
body: Something gets confused and creates Foo.java and Foo1.java, even though there is just one foo.json model.

title: [Bug]: Swagger Parser generates duplicate components with sequential suffixes when using mixed reference patterns
body: When using swagger-parser to resolve and bundle an OpenAPI specification that references external fragments, the library generates duplicate components with sequential suffixes.

title: Fix unresolved relative $ref for examples in parameter definitions
body: In OpenAPI 3.0.3 specifications, when defining parameter examples via `$ref` that point to a relative YAML file path, swagger-parser does not resolve the example correctly.

title: [Bug]: Presence of path parameter in parent reference leads to unresolved examples
body: When parsing an OpenAPI v3.0.3 specification that contains nested references for an operation with a path parameter, the examples are not resolved.

title: [Bug]: Loss of Float Precision When Reading Yaml Files
body: This happens under all versions of OpenAPI. Value loses precision when reading YAML files.

title: [OpenAPI v3.1] Arrays now (incorrectly?) parse to JsonSchema rather than ArraySchema
body: Arrays parse to JsonSchema instead of ArraySchema in 3.1.0, but parsed to ArraySchema in 3.0.x.

title: RequestBody and Response Examples with relativePath are not resolved
body: We have OpenAPI 3.0.1 spec with examples on relative path, we want to generate a single API file while creating API spec artifact.

title: create OSGi bundle artifacts
body: Create OSGi bundle artifacts instead of plain JAR similar to swagger-core.

title: [Feature]: Introduce resolveResponsesFully
body: The `resolveResponses` option has been introduced to fix the responses resolving step, but it will automatically inline responses, which we not always want to.

title: Parser ignore the description if it's a $ref
body: As said in the title, latest stable release ignores the description if it's a $ref.

title: [Openapi3.1] Ref inside of Items are not resolved
body: I have an `OrderCreate` Schema that holds properties. References inside Items are not resolved.

title: Unquoted integer `example:` value fails to parse when the spec includes a deeply nested schema elsewhere in spec
body: Unquoted integer example value fails to parse when the spec includes a deeply nested schema elsewhere.

title: Typo on main page
body: In Dealing with Let's Encrypt, "may not be sufficient on it's own", remove the apostrophe so it is instead "may not be sufficient on its own".

title: Default server URL "/" is added automatically when parsing OpenAPI 3 spec
body: When I parse an OpenAPI 3.0 document, a default server with URL "/" is automatically added if no servers are defined.

title: swagger parsers depends on NOT jakarta packages
body: After including swagger-parser as dependency I have runtime exception: java.lang.ClassNotFoundException: javax.xml.bind.annotation.XmlElement

title: Circular dependencies are not resolved properly with resolveFully
body: OpenAPIV3Parser does not seem to work as one would expect when openapi components have circular dependencies.

title: Version 2.1.28 seems to break oneOf
body: We have a Kotlin interface which gets implemented in multiple data classes. In version 2.1.28 the type got stripped.

title: Unable to Parse Referenced ArraySchema in Swagger File
body: The current swagger-parser fails to parse the referenced ArraySchema in the provided Swagger YAML file.

title: reference name is not displayed to $ref in local file
body: I want swagger to display reference name for all linked schemas in UI.

title: [OpenAPI v3]Not parsed the extensions in schema that with $ref field
body: In the parsing of schema, it is just processed the `$ref` field and return the schema Object and without processing the extension part.

title: ResolveFully doesn't resolve request body
body: The references in my requestBody is still something like a reference, they're not getting resolved properly.

title: java.lang.StackOverflowError during parsing recursive schemas/components in ResolverFully.resolveSchema()
body: Hello, OWASP ZAP fails to import some OAS that contain recursive schema.

title: openapi 3.1: Illegal character in fragment when path reference contains paramneters
body: Using `openapi-generator-maven-plugin` 7.12.0 and `swagger-parser` 2.1.25, path references with parameters cause URI syntax exception.

title: 3.1.0 spec parsing, $defs information omitted
body: 3.1.0 spec parsing, $defs information omitted

title: Parameters with the same name but different locations are incorrectly returned when using JSON references ($ref) with openapi 3.0.x.
body: Multiple parameters with the same name but different locations are wrongly handled by the parser if the parameters are described using JSON references.

title: cannot deserialize from Object value (Grallvm native executable)
body: Using plain java graalvm native executables cause multiple issues. The parser mostly returns empty OpenAPI objects without any error.

title: OpenAPIV3Parser.resolve() should handle all errors in the entire spec
body: Currently when any error occurs within resolve(), the exception is thrown and caught by try-catch. It would be perfect to collect all such errors.
